# Fix Agent Terminal Colors

## Goals

1. Agent CLIs (Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Codex) render full color output in ghostty-web terminal widgets
2. Color-killing env vars (`CI`, `NO_COLOR`) cannot leak from the host environment into agent processes

## Problem

Agent processes in ghostty-web terminals render monochrome despite the terminal supporting full color. The shell prompt renders colors fine, but agent CLIs output no color escapes. This is caused by `agentEnv()` passing `process.env` unfiltered — env vars like `CI` or `NO_COLOR` leak through and cause `supports-color` (used by all three CLIs) to disable colors.

## Approach

Harden `agentEnv()` in `terminal-welcome.js` and `cleanEnv()` in `terminal-server.js` to strip color-killing env vars and explicitly set `FORCE_COLOR=3` (truecolor).

## Files to Change

1. `packages/storyboard/src/core/cli/terminal-welcome.js` — `agentEnv()`: strip CI/NO_COLOR, add FORCE_COLOR/COLORTERM/TERM
2. `packages/storyboard/src/core/canvas/terminal-server.js` — `cleanEnv()`: strip CI/NO_COLOR; `tmuxEnv`/`directEnv`: add FORCE_COLOR

## Steps

1. Update `agentEnv()` to strip `CI`, `NO_COLOR`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS` and explicitly set `FORCE_COLOR=3`, `COLORTERM=truecolor`, `TERM=xterm-256color`
2. Update `cleanEnv()` to also strip `CI`, `NO_COLOR`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS` (add to strip regex or separate filter)
3. Add `FORCE_COLOR: '3'` to `tmuxEnv` and `directEnv` in terminal-server.js
4. Run lint + build to validate

## Edge Cases & Risks

- Stripping `CI` could affect agent behavior if agents legitimately use it — but agent CLIs don't need CI detection inside a terminal widget
- `FORCE_COLOR=3` overrides all detection — this is intentional since we control the terminal (ghostty-web supports truecolor)
